rootCauses: The validator emitted the global hotkey through System Events, which did not reliably create new stable-bundle responses on reruns.
The validator exited early on failure paths and could leave launched PushWrite.app instances running.
Safari fixture preparation could leave focus in browser chrome instead of the textarea target.
changes: Switched hotkey emission to direct HID keyboard events.
Wrapped the validator entry point in main() so defer-based cleanup also runs on failure exits.
Hardened the Safari fixture focus path for textarea-targeted validation.
control_pushwrite_product status now normalizes stale product-state.json files when the recorded PID is no longer alive.
control_pushwrite_product stop now waits for process termination and refreshes the normalized state.

stableSuccessProof.strictRule: Success requires observed target value == expected text and product response invariants to hold; product status=succeeded alone is insufficient.

openRisks: State normalization currently lives in the control path rather than inside the product runtime itself.
The Safari hardening proves the local textarea fixture path, not arbitrary web applications.
Microphone capture and whisper.cpp integration remain outside the validated scope.

rationale: Stable hotkey reruns now create fresh responses again.
Stable TextEdit and Safari runs both satisfy observedTargetValueMatches=true.

followUpTask.title: Cut microphone start/stop onto the hardened stable hotkey core
scope: Integrate real microphone start on hotkey-down and stop on hotkey-up into the stable product flow.
Extend runtime state with explicit recording and transcribing states.
Document blocked/no-mic/no-permission paths with the same strict evidence style.
Keep whisper.cpp and model integration out of scope.
